skill.name: pptx-skill
skill.description: Create, edit, and extract content from PowerPoint (.pptx) files; use when you need to generate slides programmatically, update existing decks, or export slide previews.
skill.summary: Create, edit, and extract content from PowerPoint (.pptx) files programmatically with AI assistance.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: The PPTX-Skill is a legitimate PowerPoint manipulation tool with no malicious intent. The static scanner generated 827 findings, but the vast majority are false positives triggered by code examples in markdown documentation, standard OOXML namespace URLs in XSD schemas, and XML attribute patterns misidentified as cryptographic material. Confirmed true positives include subprocess usage for LibreOffice PDF conversion (expected), ZIP file extraction for PPTX handling (required), and environment variable access for LibreOffice configuration (documented). The skill is safe to publish with appropriate warnings about external command execution.

high_findings.title: Subprocess Execution for LibreOffice
high_findings.description: scripts/office/soffice.py runs LibreOffice via subprocess.run for PPTX-to-PDF conversion, and compiles a C shared library shim using gcc when AF_UNIX sockets are unavailable. This is legitimate functionality for document conversion but requires external command execution.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: subprocess.run called with hardcoded command 'soffice' and 'gcc' - legitimate use for document conversion, no user input injection vector

high_findings.title: Archive Extraction with Zip Slip Risk
high_findings.description: Multiple scripts extract ZIP archives (PPTX files are ZIP containers) using extractall() which could be vulnerable to path traversal if the archive contains malicious entries. Risk is low since the extracted content is a known PPTX structure.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: zipfile.extractall is standard for PPTX handling but zip slip is a known pattern. Low actual risk since content is validated Office XML

high_findings.title: Subprocess Execution for PPTX Rendering
high_findings.description: scripts/thumbnail.py uses subprocess to run LibreOffice for slide rendering to images. This is expected functionality for thumbnail generation.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: subprocess.run for soffice - legitimate thumbnail rendering, no user input injection

medium_findings.title: Subprocess in Redlining Validator
medium_findings.description: scripts/office/validators/redlining.py uses subprocess.run for git diff operations. This is legitimate document comparison functionality.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: subprocess.run for git commands - legitimate use for document tracking, no user input injection

medium_findings.title: Filesystem File Write Operations
medium_findings.description: Multiple scripts write to the filesystem as part of PPTX manipulation. This is expected for a tool that creates and edits presentation files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: shutil.copy2 and file write operations for PPTX file manipulation - legitimate but noted

medium_findings.title: Archive Library Operations for PPTX Handling
medium_findings.description: Multiple scripts use zipfile and other archive libraries for standard PPTX (ZIP) file operations. Required for the skill's core functionality.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: zipfile operations for PPTX handling - legitimate use for Office file format manipulation

medium_findings.title: Hidden File Access in PPTX Directories
medium_findings.description: Scripts access _rels directories and files within PPTX structure. This is standard OOXML format access, not hidden data exfiltration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: _rels directory access is standard for OOXML format handling - legitimate use

low_findings.title: Temporary File Creation
low_findings.description: Multiple scripts create temporary files during PPTX processing. This is standard practice for file manipulation and leaves no persistent data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Tempfile creation for PPTX processing - standard pattern, no data persistence risk

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access
low_findings.description: scripts/office/soffice.py reads the process environment to configure LibreOffice. Only sets SAL_USE_VCLPLUGIN and optionally LD_PRELOAD.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: os.environ.copy() for LibreOffice configuration - legitimate use, documented in code comments

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation and License
low_findings.description: SKILL.md, LICENSE.txt, and documentation files contain hardcoded URLs pointing to GitHub repository and opensource.org. These are source references and license links.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are standard license references and source attribution - no exfiltration risk

dangerous_patterns.title: Subprocess Run without Input Validation
dangerous_patterns.description: scripts/office/soffice.py passes command-line arguments directly to subprocess.run. Arguments come from internal code, not user input, but the pattern is worth documenting.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: subprocess.run with controlled arguments - documented as expected PPTX conversion workflow

dangerous_patterns.title: Unvalidated ZIP Extraction in PPTX Handling
dangerous_patterns.description: scripts/office/unpack.py uses extractall() on PPTX files without validating archive contents. PPTX files are ZIP archives and this is standard practice, but zip slip is theoretically possible.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: extractall on PPTX archives - standard for Office file format handling, low actual zip slip risk

content.user_title: Create and Edit PowerPoint Presentations with AI
content.value_statement: Creating professional PowerPoint slides manually takes time and effort. This skill lets you generate, edit, and extract content from .pptx files programmatically using natural language instructions.
actual_capabilities: Create new .pptx presentations from structured outlines or prompts
Edit text content on existing slides including titles and body text
Insert images from local files or remote URLs into slides
Extract slide metadata such as titles for indexing and review
Duplicate and reorder slides within an existing deck
Export slides to images or PDF using external conversion tools
limitations: Cannot render slides to images natively - requires external tools like LibreOffice
Complex animations and advanced PowerPoint features may not be editable
Large decks and high-resolution images increase processing time and memory usage
Requires Python dependencies (python-pptx, Pillow, requests) to be installed

use_cases.title: Automated Report Generation
use_cases.description: Generate weekly or monthly status report presentations from structured data without manual slide creation. Ideal for recurring business reporting workflows.

use_cases.title: Template-Based Deck Creation
use_cases.description: Start from an existing company presentation template and populate it with new content. Preserves branding, layouts, and visual consistency across all slides.

use_cases.title: Slide Content Extraction and Review
use_cases.description: Extract titles and text from large presentations for indexing, search optimization, or content audit. Useful for managing presentation libraries.

prompt_templates.title: Create a New Presentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new PowerPoint presentation with 5 slides about [topic]. Include a title slide, three content slides with bullet points, and a summary slide.
prompt_templates.scenario: Generating a presentation from scratch for a new topic

prompt_templates.title: Edit an Existing Slide
prompt_templates.prompt: Open the file [file.pptx] and update slide 3. Change the title to [new title] and replace the bullet points with the following items: [list items].
prompt_templates.scenario: Updating specific slide content in an existing deck

prompt_templates.title: Generate Slides from Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the template file [template.pptx] as a starting point. Create 3 new slides using the slide layout 2 format. Title each slide based on these topics: [topics].
prompt_templates.scenario: Building slides from an existing branded template

prompt_templates.title: Extract and Summarize Slide Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the presentation [file.pptx] and extract the title of every slide. Return the results as a numbered list showing the slide order and title text.
prompt_templates.scenario: Indexing or auditing content across a large presentation

output_examples.input: Create a 3-slide presentation about machine learning basics.
output_examples.output: Generated [ml_basics.pptx]: Slide 1 - Title: Machine Learning Basics; Slide 2 - Content: Definition, Types (Supervised, Unsupervised, Reinforcement), Key Concepts; Slide 3 - Summary: ML enables computers to learn from data without explicit programming.

output_examples.input: Extract all slide titles from the quarterly report presentation.
output_examples.output: Slide titles found: 1. Q4 2024 Quarterly Report, 2. Revenue Highlights, 3. Market Expansion, 4. Product Updates, 5. Team Growth, 6. Q1 2025 Outlook, 7. Appendix: Detailed Metrics

best_practices: Use consistent slide layouts to maintain visual coherence across the full presentation.
Test with a single slide first before generating full decks to verify layout compatibility.
Keep image dimensions reasonable to avoid large file sizes and slow processing times.
anti_patterns: Do not concatenate multiple bullet points into a single paragraph - create separate XML elements for each item.
Do not use unicode bullet characters in text - use proper API parameters for bullet formatting.
Do not reuse option objects across multiple API calls as some libraries mutate objects in-place.

faq.question: What Python libraries does this skill need?
faq.answer: You need python-pptx, Pillow, and requests. Install them with: pip install python-pptx Pillow requests.

faq.question: Can I convert PPTX files to PDF?
faq.answer: Yes, but PDF conversion requires LibreOffice installed on your system. The skill uses soffice in headless mode for conversion.

faq.question: Can I edit existing slides without changing the layout?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill preserves the original slide layout and only modifies text content. Images and shapes can also be updated.

faq.question: What image formats are supported for insertion?
faq.answer: PNG, JPG, and GIF are supported. SVG images work in modern PowerPoint versions. Images are normalized to PNG for compatibility.

faq.question: Can this skill handle animated slides or transitions?
faq.answer: No. Complex animations and advanced PowerPoint features may not be editable through this skill.

faq.question: Does this work with any PowerPoint template?
faq.answer: The skill works with standard .pptx templates. Layout availability may vary by template. Test with a single slide first for compatibility.
